name: Information Technology
description: A broad program covering the design, implementation, and management of computer-based information systems with focus on networking, databases, web technologies, and IT infrastructure.

subjects.name: Programming
subjects.description: Foundational programming course covering problem solving, algorithmic thinking, control structures, functions, arrays, and basic data structures using C and Python languages.
learning_outcomes: Write structured programs using appropriate control flow constructs
Implement algorithms for searching, sorting, and basic data manipulation
Develop programs using functions, recursion, and modular design
Debug and test programs systematically
subjects.industry_relevance: Programming is the foundational skill for all IT roles including software development, DevOps, and IT automation.

subjects.name: Database Systems
subjects.description: Design and management of database systems covering relational model, SQL, normalization, transaction processing, indexing, query optimization, and introduction to NoSQL databases.
learning_outcomes: Design relational database schemas using ER diagrams and normalization
Write complex SQL queries for data manipulation and reporting
Implement transaction management and concurrency control
Compare SQL and NoSQL databases for different use cases
subjects.industry_relevance: Database skills are essential for backend development, data engineering, and IT infrastructure management.

subjects.name: Networking
subjects.description: Computer networking fundamentals covering OSI and TCP/IP models, IP addressing, routing protocols, switching, network design, and network administration with hands-on labs.
learning_outcomes: Configure routers and switches for local and wide area networks
Implement IP addressing schemes using subnetting and VLSM
Troubleshoot network connectivity issues using diagnostic tools
Design secure network topologies for enterprise environments
subjects.industry_relevance: Networking knowledge is critical for IT infrastructure roles, cloud engineering, and cybersecurity positions.

subjects.name: Operating Systems
subjects.description: Study of operating system concepts including process and thread management, memory management, file systems, I/O management, virtualization, and system administration of Windows and Linux.
learning_outcomes: Manage processes, threads, and scheduling in operating systems
Administer Linux and Windows servers including user and file management
Implement shell scripting for system automation
Configure virtual machines and containers for application deployment
subjects.industry_relevance: Operating system knowledge is fundamental for system administration, DevOps, and platform engineering roles.

subjects.name: Software Engineering
subjects.description: Systematic approach to software development covering SDLC, requirements analysis, design methodologies, testing, deployment, maintenance, and project management with Agile practices.
learning_outcomes: Apply SDLC methodologies including Agile and Waterfall models
Design software using UML diagrams and design patterns
Implement unit, integration, and system testing strategies
Manage software projects using version control and CI/CD pipelines
subjects.industry_relevance: Software engineering practices are essential for building reliable, maintainable, and scalable IT solutions.

subjects.name: Cloud Computing
subjects.description: Cloud computing technologies and services including IaaS, PaaS, SaaS models, virtualization, cloud storage, AWS/Azure/GCP platforms, and cloud application deployment and management.
learning_outcomes: Deploy and manage virtual machines and containers in cloud environments
Design cloud storage solutions using object and block storage
Implement auto-scaling and load balancing for application reliability
Manage cloud costs and optimize resource utilization
subjects.industry_relevance: Cloud computing is the dominant paradigm for IT infrastructure with most organizations migrating to cloud platforms.

subjects.name: Web Development
subjects.description: Full-stack web development covering HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, frontend frameworks (React/Angular), backend development (Node.js/Express), REST APIs, and database integration.
learning_outcomes: Build responsive web interfaces using modern HTML5 and CSS3
Develop dynamic single-page applications using React
Create RESTful APIs with Node.js and Express
Integrate databases with web applications using ORMs
subjects.industry_relevance: Web development skills are universally required for building modern business applications, e-commerce platforms, and digital services.

subjects.name: Cyber Security
subjects.description: Foundations of cybersecurity covering threat landscape, security principles, network defense, cryptography, security policies, incident response, and compliance requirements.
learning_outcomes: Identify and classify common cybersecurity threats and attack vectors
Implement network defense mechanisms including firewalls and IDS
Apply cryptographic tools for data protection
Develop incident response plans and business continuity strategies
subjects.industry_relevance: Cybersecurity is a top priority for organizations of all sizes facing increasing frequency and sophistication of cyber attacks.

subjects.name: Mobile Computing
subjects.description: Study of mobile computing technologies including mobile architectures, Android/iOS development, mobile networks, location-based services, mobile security, and cross-platform development.
learning_outcomes: Develop native mobile applications for Android using Kotlin
Build cross-platform mobile apps using Flutter or React Native
Integrate mobile apps with cloud backends and push notifications
Implement mobile security best practices for data protection
subjects.industry_relevance: Mobile computing drives digital transformation in healthcare, retail, logistics, and enterprise productivity applications.

subjects.name: Information Security
subjects.description: Comprehensive study of information security management including security governance, risk assessment, security architecture, identity management, auditing, and legal aspects of IT security.
learning_outcomes: Conduct information security risk assessments and gap analyses
Design security architecture aligning with enterprise goals
Implement identity and access management solutions
Prepare for security audits and compliance certifications
subjects.industry_relevance: Information security management is essential for compliance with regulations like GDPR, HIPAA, and SOX across all industries.
